title: Greg Lawrence

infobox.value: This article about a Saskatchewan Party member of the [Legislative Assembly of Saskatchewan](/wiki/Legislative_Assembly_of_Saskatchewan "Legislative Assembly of Saskatchewan") is a [stub](/wiki/Wikipedia:Stub "Wikipedia:Stub"). You can help Wikipedia by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Greg_Lawrence&action=edit).

(born January 29, 1966) is a [Canadian](/wiki/Canadians "Canadians") politician elected to represent the [electoral district](/wiki/Electoral_district_(Canada) "Electoral district (Canada)") of [Moose Jaw Wakamow](/wiki/Moose_Jaw_Wakamow "Moose Jaw Wakamow") in the [Legislative Assembly of Saskatchewan](/wiki/Legislative_Assembly_of_Saskatchewan "Legislative Assembly of Saskatchewan") in the [2011 election](/wiki/2011_Saskatchewan_general_election "2011 Saskatchewan general election"). He served as a member of the [Saskatchewan Party](/wiki/Saskatchewan_Party "Saskatchewan Party") until 2024.[[1]](#cite_note-1) until resigning from caucus on January 30, 2024 due to a police investigation into "historical complaints".[[2]](#cite_note-2) On January 31, 2024, Lawrence was charged with assault, along with a separate charge of assault by choking.[[3]](#cite_note-3) Lawrence had previously announced he would not run again in the [2024 election](/wiki/2024_Saskatchewan_general_election "2024 Saskatchewan general election") due to injuries sustained in a motorcycle accident.

["Sask. Party MLA Greg Lawrence resigns amid police investigation into 'historical complaints'"](https://web.archive.org/web/20240131014546/https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/saskatoon/mla-greg-lawrence-resigns-amid-police-investigation-1.7099887). Archived from [the original](https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/saskatoon/mla-greg-lawrence-resigns-amid-police-investigation-1.7099887) on 2024-01-31. Retrieved 2024-01-30.
["Moose Jaw Wakamow MLA charged with assault, choking"](https://www.discovermoosejaw.com/articles/moose-jaw-wakamow-mla-charged-with-assault). *DiscoverMooseJaw*. Retrieved 2024-02-01.

He served as a member of the [Saskatchewan Party](/wiki/Saskatchewan_Party "Saskatchewan Party") until 2024.[[1]](#cite_note-1) until resigning from caucus on January 30, 2024 due to a police investigation into "historical complaints".[[2]](#cite_note-2) On January 31, 2024, Lawrence was charged with assault, along with a separate charge of assault by choking.[[3]](#cite_note-3) Lawrence had previously announced he would not run again in the [2024 election](/wiki/2024_Saskatchewan_general_election "2024 Saskatchewan general election") due to injuries sustained in a motorcycle accident.

**Greg Lawrence** (born January 29, 1966) is a [Canadian](/wiki/Canadians "Canadians") politician elected to represent the [electoral district](/wiki/Electoral_district_(Canada) "Electoral district (Canada)") of [Moose Jaw Wakamow](/wiki/Moose_Jaw_Wakamow "Moose Jaw Wakamow") in the [Legislative Assembly of Saskatchewan](/wiki/Legislative_Assembly_of_Saskatchewan "Legislative Assembly of Saskatchewan") in the [2011 election](/wiki/2011_Saskatchewan_general_election "2011 Saskatchewan general election"). He served as a member of the [Saskatchewan Party](/wiki/Saskatchewan_Party "Saskatchewan Party") until 2024.[[1]](#cite_note-1) until resigning from caucus on January 30, 2024 due to a police investigation into "historical complaints".[[2]](#cite_note-2) On January 31, 2024, Lawrence was charged with assault, along with a separate charge of assault by choking.[[3]](#cite_note-3) Lawrence had previously announced he would not run again in the [2024 election](/wiki/2024_Saskatchewan_general_election "2024 Saskatchewan general election") due to injuries sustained in a motorcycle accident.
